# Review: deepmind/445.lean

## 1. Copyright Header
- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement
- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring
- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 445`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/445](https://www.erdosproblems.com/445)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Optional bibliography: mentions Heilbronn (unpublished) and Heath-Brown `[He00]`.

## 4. Namespace
- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos445 ... end Erdos445`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos445`).
- [x] `open Real` appears **before** the namespace block (line 32, before line 34).

## 5. Custom Definitions
- N/A — no helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements
- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_445` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- Erdős Problem 445 (OPEN): ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[He00]`.

### `answer()` usage
The problem asks "Is it true that ...?" — a yes/no question, so **Case 1** applies.
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
- [x] RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked, so `answer(True)` would mean "yes".
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)` (all `∀`/`∃` are on the RHS of `↔`), not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute
- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute
- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` (Number theory) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies
- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` multi-line format (`:= by\n  sorry`).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
- N/A — no variants in this file.

## 11. Code Quality
- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `↔`, `→`, `≡`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification
- [ ] `lake build` not run in this review.
- [ ] Custom linter checks not run in this review.

---

**Verdict:** All substantive checklist items **pass**. The file is clean, well-structured, and conforms to the style guidelines. The formalization correctly identifies the problem as a yes/no question (Case 1), places all quantifiers on the RHS of `↔`, and uses appropriate AMS classification (11 — Number theory). Build verification was not performed.

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 445
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/445](https://www.erdosproblems.com/445)
    23→
    24→Is it true that, for any $c > 1/2$, if $p$ is a sufficiently large prime then,
    25→for any $n \geq 0$, there exist $a, b \in (n, n + p^c)$ such that
    26→$ab \equiv 1 \pmod{p}$?
    27→
    28→Heilbronn (unpublished) proved this for $c$ sufficiently close to $1$.
    29→Heath-Brown [He00] used Kloosterman sums to prove this for all $c > 3/4$.
    30→-/
    31→
    32→open Real
    33→
    34→namespace Erdos445
    35→
    36→/-- Erdős Problem 445 (OPEN):
    37→Is it true that, for any $c > 1/2$, if $p$ is a sufficiently large prime then for any $n \geq 0$
    38→there exist $a, b \in (n, n + p^c)$ with $ab \equiv 1 \pmod{p}$? [He00] -/
    39→@[category research open, AMS 11]
    40→theorem erdos_445 : answer(sorry) ↔ ∀ (c : ℝ), c > 1 / 2 →
    41→    ∃ P₀ : ℕ, ∀ p : ℕ, p.Prime → P₀ ≤ p →
    42→    ∀ n : ℕ, ∃ a b : ℕ,
    43→    (n : ℝ) < (a : ℝ) ∧ (a : ℝ) < (n : ℝ) + (p : ℝ) ^ c ∧
    44→    (n : ℝ) < (b : ℝ) ∧ (b : ℝ) < (n : ℝ) + (p : ℝ) ^ c ∧
    45→    a * b ≡ 1 [MOD p] := by
    46→  sorry
    47→
    48→end Erdos445
    49→

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 445

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/445](https://www.erdosproblems.com/445)

Is it true that, for any $c > 1/2$, if $p$ is a sufficiently large prime then,
for any $n \geq 0$, there exist $a, b \in (n, n + p^c)$ such that
$ab \equiv 1 \pmod{p}$?

Heilbronn (unpublished) proved this for $c$ sufficiently close to $1$.
Heath-Brown [He00] used Kloosterman sums to prove this for all $c > 3/4$.
-/

open Real

namespace Erdos445

/-- Erdős Problem 445 (OPEN):
Is it true that, for any $c > 1/2$, if $p$ is a sufficiently large prime then for any $n \geq 0$
there exist $a, b \in (n, n + p^c)$ with $ab \equiv 1 \pmod{p}$? [He00] -/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_445 : answer(sorry) ↔ ∀ (c : ℝ), c > 1 / 2 →
    ∃ P₀ : ℕ, ∀ p : ℕ, p.Prime → P₀ ≤ p →
    ∀ n : ℕ, ∃ a b : ℕ,
    (n : ℝ) < (a : ℝ) ∧ (a : ℝ) < (n : ℝ) + (p : ℝ) ^ c ∧
    (n : ℝ) < (b : ℝ) ∧ (b : ℝ) < (n : ℝ) + (p : ℝ) ^ c ∧
    a * b ≡ 1 [MOD p] := by
  sorry

end Erdos445

content.text: The review verdict says all substantive checklist items **pass**. No changes needed.
